hints: The future of 'to be' is formed with will + be, the same for every subject.
The word 'tomorrow' signals the future tense — look for the will be form.

a1.will be: Correct: 'will be' is the standard Future Simple form of 'to be' for all subjects.
a1.will: 'will' alone is incomplete — you need 'will be' before a complement like 'at home'.
a1.am be: 'am be' mixes Present Simple 'am' with the infinitive 'be'; the future requires 'will be'.

hints: In Future Simple, 'will be' does not change for third-person subjects.
The subject 'She' does not affect the form — it is always 'will be'.

a2.will be: Correct: 'will be' is the same for I, you, he, she, it, we, they.
a2.will is: 'will is' doubles the auxiliary — after 'will', use the bare infinitive 'be', not 'is'.
a2.is will: 'is will' reverses the word order; the correct structure is 'will be'.

hints: Future Simple with 'to be': subject + will + be. No conjugation changes.
'in five minutes' is a future time marker requiring 'will be'.

a3.will be: Correct: 'will be' — uniform Future Simple form regardless of the subject.
a3.will are: 'will are' is incorrect — 'are' is a conjugated form; after 'will', use the infinitive 'be'.
a3.are will: 'are will' reverses the order; the correct form is 'will be'.

hints: Formula: subject + will + be + place/adjective.
'next Monday' is a clear future time marker.

a4.will be: Correct: 'will be' — the only valid Future Simple form of 'to be'.
a4.will been: 'been' is the past participle, not the bare infinitive; after 'will', use 'be'.
a4.is will: 'is will' has the wrong word order; the correct form is 'will be'.

hints: Weather predictions use 'will be': It will be sunny / rainy / cold.
'tomorrow' signals future tense.

a5.will be: Correct: 'will be' — the standard prediction structure: It will be + adjective.
a5.will is: 'will is' is a double auxiliary error; after 'will' use 'be'.
a5.is going: 'is going' is incomplete — even the full 'is going to be' differs from the target form 'will be'.

hints: Subject + will + be + prepositional phrase of place.
'after lunch' indicates a future time.

a6.will be: Correct: 'will be' — future location: We will be at the park.
a6.will been: 'been' is past participle; after 'will' you need the infinitive 'be'.
a6.are will: 'are will' has the wrong word order; correct is 'will be'.

hints: Negative Future Simple of 'to be': subject + will not be / won't be.
The sentence expresses a reassurance about not being late — use the negative 'won't be'.

a7.won't be: Correct: 'won't be' — the contracted negative of 'will not be'.
a7.won't: 'won't' without 'be' is incomplete — the sentence needs 'won't be' before the adjective 'late'.
a7.will not: 'will not' alone is incomplete — you still need 'be' to complete the future of 'to be'.

hints: Negative: won't be = will not be. The form is the same for all subjects.
'tonight' is a future time marker.

a8.won't be: Correct: 'won't be' — the negative Future Simple of 'to be'.
a8.isn't will: 'isn't will' mixes Present Simple negative with 'will'; correct is 'won't be'.

hints: Future Simple questions: Will + subject + be + …?
The question mark and 'this evening' signal a future question — begin with 'Will'.

a9.Will: Correct: 'Will' — Future Simple questions start with Will + subject + be.
a9.Do: 'Do' is used for Present Simple questions, not for the future of 'to be'.
a9.Are: 'Are' is Present Simple of 'be'; future questions need 'Will'.

hints: Question pattern: Will + subject + be + adjective/adverb?
'by noon' points to a specific future time.

a10.Will: Correct: 'Will' — the only option for a Future Simple question with 'be'.
a10.Does: 'Does' is Present Simple third-person; not used with future 'be'.
a10.Is: 'Is' is Present Simple; for future questions use 'Will … be'.

hints: Short answers to Will-questions: Yes, + subject + will. / No, + subject + won't. No 'be'!
In the positive short answer 'Yes, he …', use only 'will' without repeating 'be'.

a11.will: Correct: 'will' — short affirmative answers do not repeat 'be': Yes, he will.
a11.will be: 'will be' is grammatically redundant in a short answer; 'be' is already implied.
a11.is: 'is' is Present Simple; the answer to a Will-question uses 'will'.

hints: Negative short answer: No, + subject + won't. Without 'be'.
After 'No, they …', only 'won't' is needed.

a12.won't: Correct: 'won't' — short negative answer: No, they won't.
a12.won't be: 'won't be' is redundant in a short answer; 'be' is implied from the question.
a12.aren't: 'aren't' is Present Simple negative; use 'won't' for Future Simple short answers.

hints: The form 'will be' does not change regardless of the subject: My parents will be.
'at 6 PM' is a specific future time marker.

a13.will be: Correct: 'will be' — the same form for plural subjects too.
a13.will been: 'been' is the past participle; after 'will', use the infinitive 'be'.
a13.are be: 'are be' is a grammatically impossible combination; for the future, use 'will be'.

hints: To predict a future state (tired, happy, ready), use 'will be' + adjective.
'after the trip' signals a future condition.

a14.will be: Correct: 'will be' + tired — predicting a future state.
a14.will: 'will' alone is incomplete before the adjective 'tired'; you need 'will be'.
a14.are be: 'are be' is not a valid English construction; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: Weather forecast pattern: The weather will be + adjective.
'next week' is a future time marker.

a15.will be: Correct: 'will be' — standard prediction: The weather will be cold.
a15.will is: 'will is' doubles the auxiliary; after 'will' only 'be' is correct.
a15.is be: 'is be' mixes Present Simple with the infinitive; the future form is 'will be'.

hints: Affirmative Future Simple of 'to be': subject + will be + place.
'tomorrow morning' confirms the need for 'will be'.

a16.will be: Correct: 'will be' — future location: Tom will be in the office.
a16.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete before a prepositional phrase of place.
a16.is will: 'is will' has reversed word order; correct form is 'will be'.

hints: Negative: subject + won't be + adjective.
The context says the shops will NOT be open — use 'won't be'.

a17.won't be: Correct: 'won't be' — The shops won't be open on Sunday.
a17.won't: 'won't' without 'be' is incomplete before the adjective 'open'.
a17.don't be: 'don't be' is an imperative form, not Future Simple. Correct: 'won't be'.

hints: Question form: Will + it + be + adjective? For predictions.
'this afternoon' and the question mark signal a future question.

a18.Will: Correct: 'Will' — future question: Will it be warm?
a18.Does: 'Does' is Present Simple; not used for future questions with 'be'.
a18.Is: 'Is' is Present Simple of 'be'; for the future use 'Will … be'.

hints: Contraction: 'll be = will be. Used in informal and neutral English.
'next month' signals the future.

a19.'ll be: Correct: ''ll be' is the contracted form of 'will be', common in spoken English.
a19.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete; you need 'will be' or ''ll be'.
a19.are be: 'are be' is not a valid construction; for Future Simple use 'will be' or ''ll be'.

hints: Reassurances use 'will be': Everything will be fine.
'Don't worry' precedes a promise — followed by 'will be' + adjective.

a20.will be: Correct: 'will be' — a reassurance: Everything will be fine.
a20.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete before the adjective 'fine'.
a20.is be: 'is be' is not valid; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: Negative with 'until': won't be + adjective + until + time.
'until Friday' is the time marker; the negative form is 'won't be'.

a21.won't be: Correct: 'won't be' — He won't be available until Friday.
a21.won't: 'won't' without 'be' is incomplete before the adjective 'available'.
a21.doesn't be: 'doesn't be' is grammatically impossible; the negative of 'to be' in Future is 'won't be'.

hints: Question: Will + subject + be + adjective?
The question mark and 'after school' point to a future question.

a22.Will: Correct: 'Will' — Will the children be hungry?
a22.Are: 'Are' is Present Simple; for a future question use 'Will'.
a22.Do: 'Do' is not used with 'be' in questions; use 'Will … be'.

hints: Short affirmative answer: Yes, I will. No 'be'.
The question is 'Will you be free?' → short answer: Yes, I will.

a23.will: Correct: 'will' — short answer without 'be': Yes, I will.
a23.will be: 'will be' is redundant in a short answer; 'be' is implied from the question.
a23.am: 'am' is Present Simple; to answer a Will-question use 'will'.

hints: Negative short answer: No, it won't. Without 'be'.
After 'No, it …' use only 'won't'.

a24.won't: Correct: 'won't' — short negative answer: No, it won't.
a24.won't be: 'won't be' is redundant in a short answer; 'be' is already implied.
a24.isn't: 'isn't' is Present Simple negative; for a Will-question answer use 'won't'.

hints: After 'I think' use 'will be' for future opinions/predictions.
Pattern: I think + subject + will be + adjective.

a25.will be: Correct: 'will be' — prediction: I think the test will be easy.
a25.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete before the adjective 'easy'.
a25.is be: 'is be' is not valid; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: Contracted form for predictions: She'll be = She will be.
'one day' is a future time marker.

a26.'ll be: Correct: ''ll be' — She'll be a great doctor one day.
a26.is be: 'is be' is not a valid construction; use 'will be' or ''ll be'.

hints: 'will be' + adjective (closed) describes a future state.
'tomorrow' is a future time marker.

a27.will be: Correct: 'will be' + closed — future state: The museum will be closed.
a27.is be: 'is be' is not valid; the correct form is 'will be'.

hints: 'will be' does not change for plural subjects: The roads will be busy.
'during the holiday' indicates a future time.

a28.will be: Correct: 'will be' — same form for any subject in Future Simple.
a28.will are: 'will are' is incorrect — after 'will' use the infinitive 'be', not 'are'.
a28.are will: 'are will' reverses the correct order; it should be 'will be'.

hints: First Conditional main clause uses 'will be': If + Present, … will be.
'if we leave now' (Present Simple) triggers 'will be' in the main clause.

a29.will be: Correct: 'will be' — First Conditional: We will be there if we leave now.
a29.will been: 'been' is the past participle; after 'will' use the infinitive 'be'.
a29.are: 'are' is Present Simple; the main clause of a First Conditional needs 'will be'.

hints: 'will be' + ready — future state.
'when we arrive' (Present Simple after 'when') confirms 'will be' in the main clause.

a30.will be: Correct: 'will be' — future state in the main clause with 'when'.
a30.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete before the adjective 'ready'.
a30.is: 'is' is Present Simple; the main clause with 'when' for the future needs 'will be'.

hints: Negative: won't be + past participle = future passive negative.
'by next year' is a future time marker.

a31.won't be: Correct: 'won't be' — negative future passive: The bridge won't be completed.
a31.isn't be: 'isn't be' is not valid; the negative future form is 'won't be'.

hints: Question with 'there': Will there be …? — asking about future existence.
The question mark and 'there … be' structure require 'Will' at the start.

a32.Will: Correct: 'Will' — Will there be enough chairs?
a32.Are: 'Are' is Present Simple; for future 'there be' questions use 'Will'.
a32.Do: 'Do' is not used with the 'there be' construction; use 'Will there be'.

hints: After 'I promise' use 'will be' for future commitments.
'promise' signals a commitment about the future.

a33.will be: Correct: 'will be' — I promise I will be honest.
a33.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete before the adjective 'honest'.
a33.am be: 'am be' is not valid; for a future promise use 'will be'.

hints: There will be — predicting the existence of something in the future.
'a lot of people' requires the linking verb 'be' after 'will'.

a34.will be: Correct: 'will be' — There will be a lot of people.
a34.will have: 'will have' does not form the 'there is/are' construction; use 'will be'.
a34.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete in the 'there + will' pattern.

hints: Contraction ''ll be' for scheduled future actions: I'll be in a meeting.
'at 8' is a specific future time.

a35.'ll be: Correct: ''ll be' — I'll be in a meeting at 8.
a35.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete before 'in a meeting'.
a35.am: 'am' is Present Simple; for the future use 'will be' or ''ll be'.

hints: Negative in a conditional: won't be + adjective — we won't be surprised if…
'if he wins' (Present Simple) triggers 'won't be' in the main clause.

a36.won't be: Correct: 'won't be' — negative First Conditional: We won't be surprised.
a36.don't be: 'don't be' is an imperative; for First Conditional negative use 'won't be'.

hints: First Conditional: If + Present Simple, … will be. 'Would be' is for unreal conditions.
'If it rains' (Present Simple) indicates a real condition — use 'will be'.

a37.will be: Correct: 'will be' — First Conditional: If it rains, the roads will be slippery.
a37.would be: 'would be' is for Second Conditional (unreal); 'If it rains' is a real condition → 'will be'.
a37.are: 'are' is Present Simple; the main clause of First Conditional needs 'will be'.

hints: 'By this time tomorrow' points to a specific future moment.
Subject + will be + place: we will be in Paris.

a38.will be: Correct: 'will be' — future location at a specific time.
a38.are: 'are' is Present Simple; 'By this time tomorrow' requires Future.
a38.were: 'were' is Past Simple; the sentence is about the future.

hints: First Conditional: if + Present Simple → main clause with 'will be'.
'if the team works overtime' confirms the First Conditional pattern.

a39.will be: Correct: 'will be' — First Conditional: The report will be finished.
a39.would be: 'would be' is Second Conditional; here the condition is real → 'will be'.
a39.is: 'is' is Present Simple; the main clause needs 'will be'.

hints: 'will be' + adjective for a future responsibility.
'next quarter' is a future time marker.

a40.will be: Correct: 'will be' — He will be responsible for the project.
a40.will been: 'been' is past participle; after 'will' use the infinitive 'be'.
a40.is being: 'is being' is Present Continuous; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: Future Passive: will be + past participle — 'the guests will be informed'.
'informed' is a past participle; it needs 'will be' before it for Future Passive.

a41.will be: Correct: 'will be' informed — Future Passive Voice.
a41.will: 'will' without 'be' is an incomplete passive construction.
a41.are been: 'are been' is not valid; Future Passive is 'will be' + V3.

hints: 'will be' + effective — future state; 'from January' is a time marker.
Subject + will be + adjective.

a42.will be: Correct: 'will be' effective — future state with a time marker.
a42.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete before the adjective 'effective'.
a42.is been: 'is been' is not valid; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: Contraction ''ll be' for predictions: She'll be pleased.
'I'm sure' reinforces a confident prediction — 'will be' or ''ll be'.

a43.'ll be: Correct: ''ll be' — contracted prediction: She'll be pleased.
a43.is: 'is' is Present Simple; the prediction requires 'will be'.

hints: Future Passive: will be + sent (past participle).
'sent' is the V3 form; it needs 'will be' to form Future Passive.

a44.will be: Correct: 'will be' sent — Future Passive Voice.
a44.are be: 'are be' is not valid; for Future Passive use 'will be'.

hints: First Conditional: if + Present Simple → will be + adjective.
'if you skip' (Present Simple) indicates a real condition → 'will be'.

a45.will be: Correct: 'will be' — First Conditional: You will be disappointed if you skip.
a45.would be: 'would be' is for Second Conditional (unreal); here the condition is real.
a45.are: 'are' is Present Simple; the main clause needs 'will be'.

hints: 'will be' for expressing future gratitude: I will be grateful.
Subject + will be + adjective.

a46.will be: Correct: 'will be' — future gratitude: I will be grateful.
a46.am be: 'am be' is not valid; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: Future Passive: will be + announced (V3).
'next Friday' is a future time marker.

a47.will be: Correct: 'will be' announced — Future Passive with a time marker.
a47.will: 'will' without 'be' is an incomplete passive construction.
a47.are being: 'are being' is Present Continuous Passive; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: Negative with 'there': there won't be — 'there will not be' something.
'I'm afraid' precedes a negative prediction — 'won't be'.

a48.won't be: Correct: 'won't be' — there won't be any seats left.
a48.won't have: 'won't have' does not form the 'there is/are' pattern; use 'won't be'.
a48.aren't: 'aren't' is Present Simple; for a future prediction use 'won't be'.

hints: Question: Will + subject + be + adjective?
The question mark and 'accessible' signal a future question.

a49.Will: Correct: 'Will' — Will the venue be accessible?
a49.Is: 'Is' is Present Simple; for a future question use 'Will'.
a49.Does: 'Does' is not used with 'be' in questions.

hints: 'will be' for predictions: She will be the youngest person.
Subject + will be + noun phrase.

a50.will be: Correct: 'will be' — prediction: She will be the youngest.
a50.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete before a noun phrase.
a50.is been: 'is been' is not valid; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: 'Unless' = 'If … not' — First Conditional: Unless + Present, … will be.
'Unless we act now' triggers 'will be' in the main clause.

a51.will be: Correct: 'will be' — First Conditional with 'unless': the problem will be worse.
a51.would be: 'would be' is Second Conditional; 'unless' + Present → 'will be'.
a51.is: 'is' is Present Simple; the main clause needs 'will be'.

hints: Future Passive: will be + demolished (V3).
'before the year ends' confirms the future time frame.

a52.will be: Correct: 'will be' demolished — Future Passive Voice.
a52.will have: 'will have' demolished is Future Perfect Active, not Passive; for Passive use 'will be'.
a52.is: 'is' is Present Simple; for Future Passive use 'will be'.

hints: Future Passive: will be + notified (V3) — 'the staff will be notified'.
'once a decision has been made' confirms the future context.

a53.will be: Correct: 'will be' notified — Future Passive Voice.
a53.are been: 'are been' is not valid; for Future Passive use 'will be'.

hints: 'will be' for a promise: I will be more careful.
'from now on' signals a change starting now extending into the future.

a54.will be: Correct: 'will be' — a promise: I will be more careful.
a54.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete before the adjective 'careful'.
a54.am be: 'am be' is not valid; for a future promise use 'will be'.

hints: Future Passive in formal context: will be + evaluated (V3).
'on the basis of' is a formal marker; the passive needs 'will be'.

a55.will be: Correct: 'will be' evaluated — Future Passive Voice in formal register.
a55.will: 'will' without 'be' is an incomplete passive construction.
a55.are been: 'are been' is not valid; for Future Passive use 'will be'.

hints: First Conditional + negative: if + Present Simple, won't be + able to.
'If you arrive late' (Present Simple) → main clause: 'won't be'.

a56.won't be: Correct: 'won't be' — First Conditional negative: you won't be able to enter.
a56.aren't: 'aren't' is Present Simple; First Conditional main clause uses 'won't be'.

hints: Future Passive: will be + processed (V3).
'within 48 hours' specifies a future time window.

a57.will be: Correct: 'will be' processed — Future Passive Voice.
a57.will have: 'will have' processed is Future Perfect Active; for Passive use 'will be'.
a57.is: 'is' is Present Simple; for Future Passive use 'will be'.

hints: Future in the Past: after a past-tense reporting verb (assured), use 'would be' instead of 'will be'.
'assured' is Past Simple — sequence of tenses requires 'would be'.

a58.would be: Correct: 'would be' — Future in the Past after 'assured'.
a58.will be: 'will be' breaks the sequence of tenses after 'assured' (Past Simple).
a58.was: 'was' is Past Simple; here you need Future in the Past — 'would be'.

hints: 'will be' for direct predictions about the future.
'By the end of this decade' is a distant future time marker.

a59.will be: Correct: 'will be' — direct prediction about the future.
a59.would be: 'would be' is for unreal conditions or Future in the Past; this is a direct prediction.
a59.is: 'is' is Present Simple; 'By the end of this decade' requires Future.

hints: Future Passive: will be + enforced (V3).
'starting next fiscal year' confirms the future time.

a60.will be: Correct: 'will be' enforced — Future Passive Voice.
a60.are been: 'are been' is not valid; for Future Passive use 'will be'.

hints: Future Passive + guarantee: the package will be delivered.
'by Thursday' and 'I can guarantee' signal a future promise.

a61.will be: Correct: 'will be' delivered — Future Passive in a promise context.
a61.will have: 'will have' delivered is Future Perfect Active; for Passive use 'will be'.
a61.is: 'is' is Present Simple; 'by Thursday' requires Future.

hints: Unless + Present Simple → 'will be' in the main clause (First Conditional Passive).
'Unless the funding is approved' triggers 'will be' in the main clause.

a62.will be: Correct: 'will be' — First Conditional Passive: the research will be put on hold.
a62.would be: 'would be' is Second Conditional; 'unless' + Present → 'will be'.
a62.is: 'is' is Present Simple; the main clause needs 'will be'.

hints: Future Passive: will be + required (V3) — formal context.
Non-disclosure agreement — formal/legal register.

a63.will be: Correct: 'will be' required — Future Passive in formal register.
a63.are been: 'are been' is not valid; for Future Passive use 'will be'.

hints: After 'Do you think', the embedded clause keeps 'will be' + V3.
Subject + will be + conducted — Future Passive inside a question.

a64.will be: Correct: 'will be' — Future Passive: the interview will be conducted.
a64.would be: 'would be' is for unreal situations; this is a direct question.
a64.is: 'is' is Present Simple; for Future Passive use 'will be'.

hints: Future in the Past: after 'promised' (Past Simple) → 'would be'.
Sequence of tenses: promised (Past) → would be.

a65.would be: Correct: 'would be' — Future in the Past after 'promised'.
a65.will be: 'will be' breaks the sequence of tenses after 'promised' (Past).
a65.is: 'is' is Present Simple; here you need Future in the Past.

hints: Future Passive: will be + renovated (V3).
'by the time the company moves in' marks a future deadline.

a66.will be: Correct: 'will be' renovated — Future Passive Voice.
a66.will have: 'will have' starts Future Perfect Active; for Passive use 'will be'.
a66.would be: 'would be' is for unreal conditions; this is a direct prediction.

hints: Future in the Past: after 'warned' (Past Simple) → 'would be'.
'warned' triggers sequence of tenses → 'would be'.

a67.would be: Correct: 'would be' — Future in the Past after 'warned'.
a67.will be: 'will be' breaks the sequence of tenses after 'warned' (Past).
a67.are: 'are' is Present Simple; here you need Future in the Past.

hints: 'will be' + mandatory — future obligation in formal context.
Subject + will be + adjective.

a68.is been: 'is been' is not valid; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: First Conditional Passive: if + Present Simple → will be + V3.
'If the merger goes through' (Present) → 'will be' restructured.

a69.will be: Correct: 'will be' restructured — First Conditional Passive.
a69.would be: 'would be' is Second Conditional; 'if' + Present → 'will be'.
a69.is: 'is' is Present Simple; the main clause needs 'will be'.

hints: Future in the Past: after 'confirmed' (Past Simple) → 'would be'.
'confirmed' triggers sequence of tenses → 'would be'.

a70.would be: Correct: 'would be' — Future in the Past after 'confirmed'.
a70.will be: 'will be' breaks the sequence of tenses after 'confirmed'.
a70.is: 'is' is Present Simple; here you need Future in the Past.

hints: Future Passive in formal register: will be + held accountable.
Subject + will be + V3.

a71.will be: Correct: 'will be' held — Future Passive in formal register.
a71.are been: 'are been' is not valid; for Future Passive use 'will be'.

hints: 'will be' + applicable — future state in formal documents.
Subject + will be + adjective.

a72.will be: Correct: 'will be' applicable — formal future state.
a72.is been: 'is been' is not valid; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: Formal prediction: 'It is anticipated that' + subject + will be + V3.
'It is anticipated that' signals a formal forecast.

a73.will be: Correct: 'will be' eliminated — Future Passive after 'It is anticipated that'.
a73.would be: 'would be' is for unreal conditions; 'It is anticipated' expresses confidence → 'will be'.
a73.is: 'is' is Present Simple; for Future Passive use 'will be'.

hints: Formal inversion: 'Should' + subject + infinitive = 'If' + Present Simple → 'will be'.
'Should the proposal gain approval' is a formal First Conditional inversion.

a74.will be: Correct: 'will be' — First Conditional with formal 'Should' inversion.
a74.would be: 'would be' is for unreal conditions; formal 'Should' + infinitive → 'will be'.
a74.are: 'are' is Present Simple; the main clause needs 'will be'.

hints: 'will be' + far-reaching — predicting a future state in formal register.
Subject + will be + adjective.

a75.will: 'will' without 'be' is incomplete before the adjective.
a75.are been: 'are been' is not valid; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: Formal expression: 'It will be incumbent upon' = it will be the duty of.
'It' + 'will be' + adjective + 'upon' — formal register.

a76.will be: Correct: 'will be' — formal: It will be incumbent upon the board.
a76.would be: 'would be' is for unreal conditions; this is a direct statement.
a76.is been: 'is been' is not valid; for the future use 'will be'.

hints: Future Passive: will be + maintained (V3).
